unit.note: F0 fresh gate green. Server vitest 166 files / 1924 passed + 1 skipped (1925) (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + tsc --noEmit clean + npm run build green. New F17 coverage in server/src/terminal/spawn-env.test.ts (6 tests): PORT strip, VITE_PORT+HONO_HOST strip, caller-cannot-re-leak (strip runs AFTER the merge), unrelated-vars-survive incl. the SUPPORT_EMAIL substring-of-PORT guard + SHIPWRIGHT_MONOREPO_PATH, full parent-session-strip contract intact under the port-strip (CHILD_SESSION/SESSION_ID/ENTRYPOINT/CLAUDECODE), and a non-network SHIPWRIGHT_* knob (MAX_CONCURRENT) still inheritable. AC2 proven RED-first on pre-fix main (5/5 failed: PORT/VITE_PORT/HONO_HOST spread through the pty env -> PORT-in-env true), green after. The existing pty-env-flicker.test.ts (22) stays green -> no flicker/color/marker regression. Client workspace NOT re-run: no client/** file changed (detect_frontend_changes.py has_frontend_changes=false); the change is confined to server/src/terminal/spawn-env.ts + its co-located test, outside the client workspace.

integration.note: Confidence-calibration probes of the touches_io_boundary env boundary (producer = webui production launchers stamping PORT + the process.env spread -> consumer = the spawned pty child shell / a nested dev server). (1) Windows round-trip probe (real, on this Win11 box): PORT=3847 VITE_PORT=5173 HONO_HOST=true node -e spread-then-exact-delete -> the three keys spread as canonical UPPERCASE and exact-delete removes ALL three -> the launcher-stamped casing is handled; no finding. (2) RED-first regression probe: the new suite is 5/5 RED on pre-fix main (leak reproduced), GREEN after. (3) Call-site probe: the SOLE runtime caller pty-manager.spawn() calls spawnFn(shell,[],{cwd,cols,rows}) with NO env field -> opts.env is always undefined -> strip-after-merge breaks no real caller (callerEnv is a test-only escape hatch); no finding. Two consecutive clean rounds (probe 1 + probe 3) => asymptote reached, boundary calibrated. Edge not probed: case-variant injection (Port) - accepted, no launcher emits it (probe 1 covers the canonical case).

e2e.note: No Playwright run. This iterate touches ONLY server/src/terminal/spawn-env.ts (a pure env-map factory) + its co-located unit test - no client/src file, no DOM/route/component, no Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS surface. detect_frontend_changes.py reports has_frontend_changes=false (nothing under client/**). F0.5 surface=none (see surface_verification); a browser smoke would exercise nothing this env-strip affects - env-var stripping is only observable by spawning a real pty and inspecting its child env, which the unit test does directly against buildSpawnEnv.

surface_verification.justification: This iterate changes only server/src/terminal/spawn-env.ts (the pure buildSpawnEnv env-map factory for the embedded-terminal pty) + its co-located unit test. No file under client/src is touched - detect_frontend_changes.py reports has_frontend_changes=false, so there is genuinely no browser/DOM/route/Vite-config/proxy/WS/CORS surface to verify. The behavior (the pty env must NOT inherit the webui's PORT/VITE_PORT/HONO_HOST) is only observable by inspecting a spawned child's env, which the Vitest suite does directly against buildSpawnEnv (6 tests, AC2 RED-first on pre-fix main: PORT/VITE_PORT/HONO_HOST leaked -> 5/5 failed, green after) plus the empirical Windows process.env round-trip calibration probe. F0.5 surface=none - no fabricated browser run.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 - buildSpawnEnv strips the webui's own PORT/VITE_PORT/HONO_HOST so a PORT-honouring dev server started in the embedded terminal no longer collides with the webui (production launchers stamp PORT=3847).
behaviors.evidence: spawn-env.test.ts: with base env PORT=3847/VITE_PORT=5173/HONO_HOST=true, the returned env lacks all three; a WEBUI_OPERATIONAL_ENV_KEYS strip loop deletes them AFTER the base+caller merge (mirroring PARENT_SESSION_ENV_KEYS).

behaviors.behavior: AC2 - a NEW regression test is RED on pre-fix main and GREEN after.
behaviors.evidence: RED captured on pre-fix main (no strip loop): 5/5 new tests failed - PORT-in-env expected false, received true (PORT/VITE_PORT/HONO_HOST spread through). GREEN after the fix: vitest spawn-env.test.ts + pty-env-flicker.test.ts -> 28 passed.

behaviors.behavior: AC3 - full server suite + build stay green; no invariant/ADR regressed (ADR-067 shell-only whitelist, SHIPWRIGHT_WEBUI marker, CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER default-ON, parent-session strip all intact).
behaviors.evidence: Server vitest 166 files / 1924 passed + 1 skipped + tsc + build green; the 22 pty-env-flicker.test.ts contracts stay green. Client not re-run (no client/** change). Read-only-observer rules 1/12 intact.

behaviors.behavior: AC4 - files stay within the 2-file footprint; bloat baseline not ratcheted.
behaviors.evidence: Exact footprint: server/src/terminal/spawn-env.ts (201 -> 234) + new server/src/terminal/spawn-env.test.ts (136) - both < 300, spawn-env.ts is NOT in shipwright_bloat_baseline.json (no new crossing, baseline unchanged). Serial #12, deps/blocks none, D01-D11 merged -> no live parallel collision.
